general.scannerCacheDescription: Remove cached vulnerability databases to free disk space. Next scan will re-download fresh data (~200MB).
general.activityEventCollectionModeTooltip: Stream: Continuous event stream from Docker, instant notifications, higher CPU usage. Poll: Periodic checks for new events, slight notification delay, lower CPU usage.
general.metricsCollectionIntervalTooltip: How often to collect CPU/memory metrics from running containers. Lower intervals provide more frequent updates but increase CPU usage.
general.scheduleExecutionCleanupDescription: Delete executions older than specified days
general.containerEventCleanupDescription: Delete events older than specified days
general.volumeHelperCleanupDescription: Automatically removes temporary containers used for browsing volume contents. Runs every 30 minutes and on startup.
general.scannerCacheCleanupDescription: Remove cached vulnerability databases to reclaim disk space
general.labelFilterMatchingTooltip: Controls how multiple selected labels filter environments on the dashboard. Any: shows environments that have at least one of the selected labels. All: shows only environments that have every selected label.

updates.usedImagesPreserved: Images in use by running or stopped containers will never be removed.
updates.timezoneDescription: Used for scheduling auto-updates, git syncs, and image pruning

gitDeploy.title: Git deploy
gitDeploy.confirmPrefix: This will pull the latest changes for
gitDeploy.confirmSuffix: Containers will only restart if the configuration changed.

gitModal.descriptionCreate: Deploy a compose stack from a Git repository
gitModal.noRepositories: No repositories configured. Click "Add new" to add one.
gitModal.stackNameHelp: This will be the name of the deployed stack
gitModal.composePathHelp: Path to the compose file within the repository
gitModal.envFileTooltipDefault: A .env file in the compose directory is always loaded automatically, if present.
gitModal.envFileTooltipAdditional: Use this field for an additional env file with a non-standard name, such as .env.production. Its values override the default .env.
gitModal.envFileTooltipOverrides: Overrides from the environment variables editor on the right always take highest precedence.
gitModal.additionalEnvFileHelp: Additional env file to pass to Docker Compose
gitModal.contextTooltipWorkingDirectory: Working directory for Docker Compose, relative to the repository root. All files in this directory will be available for volume mounts and build contexts.
gitModal.contextTooltipRoot: Use . for the repository root when your compose file references files in sibling directories.
gitModal.contextTooltipDefault: Defaults to the compose file's parent directory.
gitModal.contextHelp: Relative to repository root, e.g. . for root
gitModal.enableScheduledSyncHelp: Automatically sync repository and redeploy stack if there are changes.
gitModal.enableWebhookHelp: Receive push events from your Git provider to trigger sync and redeploy.
gitModal.webhookSecretPlaceholder: Leave empty for no signature verification
gitModal.webhookUrlAfterCreate: The webhook URL will be available after creating the stack.
gitModal.webhookConfigureHelp: Configure this URL in your Git provider. Secret is used for signature verification.
gitModal.buildImagesOnDeployHelp: Run --build to build images from Dockerfiles before starting containers.
gitModal.disableBuildCacheHelp: Pass --no-cache to force a clean build without using cached layers.
gitModal.repullImagesHelp: Always pull latest images before deploying, even if the compose file hasn't changed. Useful for CI/CD workflows with static tags like :latest.
gitModal.forceRedeploymentHelp: Always redeploy the stack on webhook or scheduled sync, even if no git changes are detected.
gitModal.deployNowHelp: Clone and deploy the stack immediately
gitModal.envOverridesInfo:
Override variables from your repository env files. Non-secrets are saved to .env.dockhand in the stack directory. Secrets are stored in the database and injected via shell environment at deploy time.

Variables are available for compose file interpolation using ${VAR_NAME} syntax. They are not automatically injected into containers; use environment: or reference .env.dockhand in env_file: to pass them through.
gitModal.populateTooltip: Clone the repository and load environment variables from the .env file in the compose directory and the additional env file, if specified, so you can see what you can override.
gitModal.existsDescription: A stack named "{name}" already exists. Please choose a different name.

validation.stackNameInvalid: Stack name must start with a letter or number, and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores

toasts.enterRepositoryUrlFirst: Please enter a repository URL first
toasts.noEnvFoundDescription: No .env files found in the repository. You can still add variables manually.
toasts.loadedVariablesDescription: You can now customize values before deploying

modal.descriptionCreate: Create a new Docker Compose stack

edges.dependencyDescription: This service depends on {source} and will start after it.

import.browserDescription: Browse to a compose file or scan a directory for stacks.
import.remoteDescription: Browse the Dockhand host filesystem to find compose files. Files are managed locally; Hawser only proxies Docker API calls, not filesystem access.
import.foundDescription: {count} stack(s) found. Select which ones to adopt into {environment}.
import.remoteFilesPrefix: These compose files are on the
import.remoteFilePrefix: This compose file is on the
import.remoteFilesSuffix: Docker commands will be sent to {environment} via Hawser, but the files are managed locally.
import.remoteFileSuffix: Docker commands will be sent to {environment} via Hawser, but the file is managed locally.
import.adoptManyInfo: Dockhand will track these compose files, letting you edit, start, and stop the stacks from the UI. Your files stay in their current location.
import.adoptOneInfo: Dockhand will track this compose file, letting you edit, start, and stop the stack from the UI. Your files stay in its current location.
import.previewDescription: Review the compose file before adopting.

toasts.allAlreadyAdopted: All {count} stack(s) in this directory are already adopted
toasts.noneFound: No compose stacks found in this directory

layout.switchToVertical: Switch to vertical layout (logs on side)

tooltips.untrackedStack: Compose file location unknown. Click the stack name or edit button to locate it.

schedules.title: Schedules
schedules.emptyDescription: Enable auto-update on containers or auto-sync on git stacks to see them here

scheduleExecutionCleanup.name: Schedule execution cleanup
scheduleExecutionCleanup.description: Removes execution logs older than {days} days

containerEventCleanup.name: Container event cleanup
containerEventCleanup.description: Removes container events older than {days} days

volumeHelperCleanup.name: Volume helper cleanup
volumeHelperCleanup.description: Cleans up temporary volume browser containers

scannerCacheCleanup.name: Scanner cache cleanup
scannerCacheCleanup.description: Removes scanner vulnerability database cache to reclaim disk space

batchOperation.runningCloseConfirm: Operation is still running. Cancel and close?

errors.itemsRequired: Items array is required and must not be empty

errors.failedToGetNewImageId: Failed to get new image ID after pull

blockReasons.any: Found {total} vulnerabilities ({critical} critical, {high} high, {medium} medium, {low} low)
blockReasons.criticalHigh: Found {critical} critical and {high} high severity vulnerabilities
blockReasons.moreThanCurrent: New image has {total} vulnerabilities vs {current} in current image

avatarCropper.cropHelp: Drag to reposition. Use the slider to zoom.

test.title: Dockhand test notification
test.message: This is a test notification from Dockhand. If you receive this, your notification settings are configured correctly.

disk.absoluteMessage: Environment "{environment}" is using {used} of Docker disk space (threshold: {threshold} GB)
disk.percentageMessage: Environment "{environment}" Docker disk usage is at {percent}% ({used} used)

license.expiringMessage: Your {licenseType} license expires in {days} day(s) ({date}). Contact support to renew.

vulnerability.highMessage: Image "{image}" has {count} high severity vulnerabilities ({total} total)

autoUpdate.successMessage: Container "{container}" was updated to a new image version

gitSync.skippedMessage: Stack "{stack}" sync skipped: no changes detected
gitSync.successMessage: Stack "{stack}" was synced and deployed successfully

batchUpdate.allFailedMessage: Update failed for all containers in {environment}

imagePrune.successMessage: {count} unused images removed, {space} disk space reclaimed

noEnvironment.selectedDescription: Select a Docker environment from the dropdown to get started
noEnvironment.configuredDescription: Add a Docker environment in Settings to get started

interpolation.full: These variables are available for compose file interpolation using ${VAR_NAME} syntax. To pass them to containers, reference them in the compose file's environment: section.

secrets.storage: Secrets are never written to disk and are injected via shell environment when the stack starts.

parseWarnings.title: Some lines couldn't be parsed:
parseWarnings.switchToText: Switch to text view to edit these lines.
